# Week 8

## Materials

📕 [Class slides]({{< var spring-week8-class-slides >}})

🗂️ [Presentations shared folder]({{< var final-presentation-2-shared-folder >}})

🔄 [Feedback grid for Block 1]({{< var final-presentation-2-feedback-forms-block1 >}})

🔄 [Feedback grid for Block 2]({{< var final-presentation-2-feedback-forms-block2 >}})

#### Final presentations

📗 [Final presentations logistics slides - Jamie Montgomery, MEDS Program Director]({{< var final-presentations-logistics-slides >}})

📙 [Final presentations schedule]({{< var public-presentations-schedlue >}})

📗 [Ten ways to improve presentations - Dr. Alexandra Phillips, Asst. Teaching Professor]({{< var ten-ways-to-improve-presentations-workshop >}})

📙 [Responding to questions - Dr. Allison Horst]({{< var allison-horst-responding-questions >}})

## Schedule

The class will be divided in two blocks from 1:10 to 2:30 and from 2:30 to 4:00. Each team will present and provide feedback to other teams as follows:

![](/411b_spring/images/presentations_blocks_2.png)

Your theam should work on their project outside of their "presentation block". The following rooms are reserved for you so you have a dedicated workspace during our usual class time. You’re welcome to use other places to work. 

![](/411b_spring/images/presentations_rooms_2.png)

## Upcoming deadlines

{{< include _spring_deadlines_tables.qmd >}}

<div id="table-week8-10"></div>


## To do by next week


### 1. 📊 Complete data archival

**Due when?** {{< var data-archival-deadline >}} is the final due date to archive your capstone project data. This is an academic requirement.

**How to submit:**  Submit data's DOI or explain why there is no data archival using [this Google form]({{< var data-archival-form >}}).

<details>
<summary>**Instructions**</summary>

You should have already:

1. Discussed with your advisor or client what data (if any) should be archived as part of the project deliverables.
2. If necessary, made an appointment with the RDS team (rds@library.ucsb.edu) to discuss archival of data associated with your capstone project. 

Before contacting the Library RDS team or archiving your data, please read through the [Archiving and Preserving Your Data]({{< var RDS-data-archival-guide >}}) information for detailed guidelines about what to archive and the submission process to Dryad. 

When you submit data to Dryad (recommended repository) the data archival process won’t be complete yet, as your submission might need to be revised. However, you will get a DOI associated with your data. **To fulfill the data archival requirement, you must [submit your data’s DOI]({{< var data-archival-form >}}) by {{< var data-archival-deadline >}}. If you are not archiving any data related to your capstone project, you must briefly explain why that is the case.**


</details>

<br>



### 2. 💬 Submit editable file of closed captioning script

**Due when?**  Due on {{< var editable-closed-caption-deadline >}} at midnight.

**How to submit:** Upload as a Word (.doc) to [this Box folder]({{< var final-presentations-cc-scripts-box-folder >}}). Not PDF: must be an editable text file.

<details>
<summary>**Instructions**</summary>
- Don’t need to create a full script but submit it if you have one; outline or working notes are fine.
- Provide as much information as possible about what you will be saying in your presentation to assist the live closed captioner and increase caption accuracy
- All proper names and technical words at minimum, but more detail is helpful
- Word (.doc) format preferred, **not PDF**, can even be slide notes (must be editable text).
</details>

<br>

### 3. 🎤  Prepare final presentation and Q&A 

**Due when?** Due by next class Wednesday, May 28.

**How to submit:** Add slides to [shared drive]({{< var final-presentation-3-shared-folder >}}).

<details>
<summary>**Instructions**</summary>

- Incorporate the feedback you received today and streamline your presentation
- *Practice at least once with your faculty advisor*
- Prepare for Q&A (check class slides!)
- Schedule extra practice presentations or feedback sessions with TA.
- You've got this! 

</details>

<br>

### 4. 🧰 Update Technical Documentation and clean repositories
**Due when?** Due last day of quarter, {{< var final-academic-delierables-deadline >}}.

<details>
<summary>**Instructions**</summary>
- Discuss feedback with your team
- Start working on updating your TD and repositories
- Contact advisors or clients for clarification and reply to comments within the document as needed. 
- Update GitHub repositories 
- Final versions due in two weeks! 
</details>

<br>

### 5. 💥 Keep implementing your project!
<center>
![](/411b_spring/images/coding.gif){width=35%}
</center>


## Schedule for next class
:::{.callout-important}
## Remember: updated schedule next week!
There is no class on Monday. We will have class on {{< var spring-week9-class-date >}} at BH 1414.
:::


![](/411b_spring/images/presentations_blocks_3.png)
